title: Project Setup and Static Data Layer
summary: Initialize Next.js App Router project with Tailwind CSS, configure Polish locale, and create the static data source.
acceptanceCriteria:
- Next.js project initialized with Tailwind CSS and App Router.
- `html lang="pl"` set in `app/layout.tsx`.
- `lib/data.ts` created with static mock data for cats, shelter statistics, donation info, and contact details.
- Basic project builds successfully.

title: Global Layout, Header, and Footer
summary: Create the persistent layout components including a responsive header with smooth scrolling navigation and a footer.
acceptanceCriteria:
- `Header` component includes a sticky navigation bar and a mobile hamburger menu toggle.
- Navigation links point to `#adopcja`, `#wsparcie`, `#wolontariat`, `#kontakt`.
- `Footer` includes copyright info, social media links, and a link to the privacy policy.
- Both components are integrated into `app/layout.tsx`.

title: Hero and About Us Sections
summary: Implement the top sections of the landing page: a welcoming Hero with CTAs and an About section with shelter statistics.
acceptanceCriteria:
- `Hero` section displays a welcoming Polish headline, a placeholder background image, and primary CTAs for 'Adoptuj' and 'Wesprzyj nas'.
- `About` section displays the shelter's mission statement, brief history, and key statistics loaded from `lib/data.ts`.
- Both sections are added to `app/page.tsx`.

title: Adoption Gallery Section
summary: Build the responsive grid of available cats using standard UI cards.
acceptanceCriteria:
- `CatCard` component created to display cat image (with `next/image` and alt text), name, age, and short description.
- `AdoptionGallery` section maps over the static cat data to render a responsive CSS grid of `CatCard`s.
- The section has the ID `adopcja` for smooth scrolling and is added to `app/page.tsx`.

title: Support and Volunteer Sections
summary: Implement the sections detailing how to support the shelter financially, materially, and through volunteering.
acceptanceCriteria:
- `Support` section (ID `wsparcie`) displays bank account details and lists needed items based on data.
- `Volunteer` section (ID `wolontariat`) displays instructions on how to become a volunteer and includes a simple, client-side only contact/signup form.
- Form submissions are simulated to show a 'DziÄ™kujemy' success message.
- Both sections are integrated into `app/page.tsx`.

title: Contact Section and Final Polish
summary: Implement the Contact section and perform final checks for responsiveness, accessibility, and smooth scrolling.
acceptanceCriteria:
- `Contact` section (ID `kontakt`) displays address, opening hours, phone, email, and a basic contact form with a simulated success message.
- Smooth scrolling works seamlessly across all navigation links from the header.
- The site is fully responsive on mobile, tablet, and desktop.
- Lighthouse checks indicate high performance and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance (e.g., correct color contrasts and alt texts on all images).
